relations.note: Coral usage constraint refines the tertiary-as-accent principle of the palette hierarchy.

quotes: Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

rules.intent: Preserve the intended brand color balance.
rules.rule_text: Apply the palette in the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

quotes: **Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
**Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).

rules.intent: Ensure legible, on-brand text color per background.
rules.rule_text: Headlines/headings use Brand Blue (headline.color = #00529b), or White on dark/gradient backgrounds. Body text uses Graphite (body.color = #212121) on light backgrounds and White on dark backgrounds, with increased line height (line_height.light_on_dark = 160%) per §1.2.

quotes: **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**
Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.

rules.intent: Keep gold/sunshine meaningful as a data emphasis cue.
rules.rule_text: In charts, Brand Blue (#00529b) + Sky Blue (#358CCB) are dominant; Sunshine (#faa31b)/Gold (#ffc60a) are strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures; gray is used for placebo.

quotes: **Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
**Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Standardize callout surface tinting by callout type.
rules.rule_text: Callout surfaces use Light Blue (#E6F0F9) at 70% tint for data callouts (callout.data.fill / callout.data.fill.opacity = 0.7) and Golden Sand (#FEF1C8) at 30% tint for text-heavy callouts (callout.text_heavy.fill / callout.text_heavy.fill.opacity = 0.3).

effect.reserved_for: sparing tertiary accent only — never primary CTAs, headlines, or data
effect.subject: color.tertiary.coral

quotes: **Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data.
**Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

rules.intent: Keep Coral a rare accent, not a primary or data color.
rules.rule_text: Coral (#F26A38) is a sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. [GENERAL] Avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

quotes: **Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Consistent, legible link styling across backgrounds.
rules.rule_text: Links are Brand Blue (body.link.color = #00529b), underlined (body.link.decoration = underline) on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark backgrounds.

quotes: The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

rules.intent: Preserve trademark integrity of the logo mark treatment.
rules.rule_text: The logo icon's 70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).
